summary: Epic Scarlet>Fire, Terrapin Station, and tight jams define this '79 Nassau show
blurb: The concert shines thanks to a monumental Scarlet Begonias → Fire On The Mountain segue and a soaring Terrapin Station. Brent Mydland’s keyboards are on fire throughout, especially during the second‑set jam. Overall the setlist flows flawlessly with strong first‑set work and an inspired second‑set climax.
review: On November 1, 1979 at Nassau Coliseum the Dead delivered one of the most memorable performances of their fall ’79 tour. The show opens solidly with Jack Straw and They Love Each Other before building momentum through Mexicali Blues, Peggy‑O and Candyman, but it’s the second set that truly elevates the night. A seven‑minute Scarlet Begonias → Fire On The Mountain transition erupts into a jam that reviewers call “out there” and “one of the best ever,” with Jerry Garcia soaring on wah‑filled leads while Brent Mydland fuels the groove with synth‑funk textures and fiery organ work. From there the band rides into a majestic Terrapin Station, rolls into an expansive Playing In The Band, and drives through a tight Rhythm Devils segment that culminates in a powerful Black Peter → Good Lovin' finish before a classic U.S. Blues encore. Phil Lesh’s bass stays rock‑solid throughout, Mickey Hart and Bill Kreutzmann lock in with flawless percussion, and Bob Weir adds inspirational rhythm and vocal flair. The soundboard recordings capture the energy clearly despite occasional minor dropouts, making this night a must‑listen for any Deadhead seeking that quintessential late‑’70s magic.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Strong positive sentiment across all recordings: multiple reviewers call the Scarlet>Fire jam "mind‑blowing" and "one of the best ever," praise Brent Mydland’s keyboards, and highlight tight band playing throughout. No consistent negative comments were found.

best_recording.reason: Clear soundboard capture with minimal dropouts, preserving the legendary Scarlet>Fire transition and overall band dynamics.

key_highlights: Monumental Scarlet Begonias → Fire On The Mountain segue that defines the second set
Brent Mydland’s keyboard work is "ON FIRE" throughout, especially during the jam sections
Terrapin Station and Playing In The Band deliver expansive, melodic journeys
Tight rhythm section with Mickey Hart and Bill Kreutzmann driving a flawless Rhythm Devils segment

band_performance.Jerry: Shows strong, soaring solos on Scarlet>Fire and Good Lovin', with occasional early‑mix low levels but overall a standout performance.
band_performance.Phil: Delivers tight, vibrant bass throughout; consistently praised for holding down the groove and being "right there" with Jerry.
band_performance.Bob: Provides inspirational rhythm guitar and vocal contributions, part of the tight ensemble that drives the show forward.
band_performance.Brent: Keyboard work is a major highlight – organ, Rhodes, and synth‑funk textures blaze on Scarlet>Fire and throughout the set, plus strong vocal moments on Easy To Love You.
band_performance.Mickey: Solid drumming that underpins the jams; noted for adding a country‑rock flavor to Scarlet>Fire and maintaining steady drive.
band_performance.Billy: Combined with Mickey delivers expert Rhythm Devil work, especially during Space and the powerful Black Peter → Good Lovin' climax.
